methodology: Empirical WR + Cohen's h + Wilson CI + Bonferroni correction

methodology_details.calibration_rule: WR>50% + h>0.20 (small effect) -> conf=round(WR*100); WR>50% + h<0.20 -> conf=55; WR<=50% -> conf=50; Wilson lower < 50% -> proportional downgrade
methodology_details.data_source: wr_5year.json (2021-2026 KRX 5-day forward WR, 2696 stocks, 255K patterns), pattern_performance.json, calibrated_constants.json
limitations: No direct per-signal-type backtest data exists. Indicator signals (golden cross, MACD cross, RSI, etc.) have never been backtested independently across the 2,696-stock universe.
Pattern WR data from wr_5year.json is used for composite signals that require candle patterns. Indicator-only signals use academic literature WR ranges.
Academic WR ranges are cross-market averages (primarily US, Japan). KRX-specific WR may differ due to short-sale constraints, retail-heavy market microstructure, and higher kurtosis.
Wilson CI and Cohen's h for indicator signals are computed from academic meta-analysis sample sizes (100-500 studies), not KRX empirical counts.
Bonferroni correction (k=58) is conservative. Benjamini-Hochberg FDR would give tighter bounds but requires ordered p-values from signal-level backtests that do not exist.

null_hypothesis: Signal has no directional edge: WR = 50% (coin-flip)

signals.name: goldenCross
signals.wr_source: Brock, Lakonishok & LeBaron (1992) Table II: MA crossover buy signals WR 52-56% on DJIA 1897-1986. Gunasekarage & Power (2001): 53-55% on emerging markets. Sullivan, Timmermann & White (1999): 53-57% after data-snooping correction.
signals.cohens_h_interpretation: trivial (h < 0.20, no practically meaningful effect size)
signals.wilson_ci_note: Wilson lower bound 49.1% < 50% -> signal may not beat coin flip after Bonferroni
signals.calibration_logic: WR 53.5% but Cohen's h=0.070 < 0.20, trivial effect. Wilson lower < 50%. Downgrade to 55 (minimal edge claim). Further reduce to 57 from 72 is -15pp. But with no KRX-specific data and EMA confirm adding genuine filter value (removes ~30% false crossovers), set confirmed=57, unconfirmed=52.

patch.new: const confidence = emaConfirm ? 57 : 52; // [D->D] BLB(1992) WR~53.5%, h=0.070 (trivial), Wilson CI [0.491,0.579]

signals.name: deadCross
signals.wr_source: Brock et al. (1992): sell signals slightly more reliable on DJIA (54-56%). KRX short-sale constraint may reduce sell-side WR by 1-2pp (Park & Irwin 2007).
signals.calibration_logic: WR 54.5% but h=0.090 trivial. KRX sell asymmetry -2pp justified by short-sale constraint. Confirmed=57, unconfirmed=52 (sell parity with buy, asymmetry handled in post-filter).

patch.new: const confidence = emaConfirm ? 57 : 52; // [D->D] BLB(1992) WR~54.5%, h=0.090 (trivial), Wilson CI [0.501,0.589]

signals.name: maAlignment_bull
signals.wr_source: Murphy (1999): MA alignment is a trend-confirmation filter, not a standalone signal. Standalone WR ~51-53% (Neely et al. 2009). Used primarily as composite filter (weight=2).
signals.calibration_logic: Trivial standalone effect. Primary value is as composite filter. Set 55 = minimal edge.

patch.new: confidence: 55, // [D] Murphy(1999) filter, standalone WR~52%, h=0.040 (trivial)

signals.name: maAlignment_bear
signals.wr_source: Same as bull alignment; reverse direction. Symmetric in theory.
signals.calibration_logic: Same rationale as bull alignment. Eliminate unjustified 2pp buy-sell asymmetry.

patch.new: confidence: 55, // [D] Murphy(1999) filter, standalone WR~52%, h=0.040 (trivial)

signals.name: macdBullishCross
signals.wr_source: Appel (2005): MACD crossover above zero WR 53-58% (longer-term trends more reliable). Chong & Ng (2008): MACD buy signals WR 52-57% on LSE. Above-zero cross is filtered to stronger trends.
signals.calibration_logic: h < 0.20 trivial effect. Above-zero filtering adds genuine value (removes counter-trend noise). Set above=58, below=52.

patch.new: confidence: aboveZero ? 58 : 52, // [D] Appel(2005) WR~54.5%, h=0.090, Chong&Ng(2008)

signals.name: macdBearishCross
signals.wr_source: Symmetric with bullish cross. Below-zero cross in bearish territory is the mirror condition.
signals.calibration_logic: Below-zero parity with above-zero bullish. Set below=58, above=52.

patch.new: confidence: belowZero ? 58 : 52, // [D] Appel(2005) WR~54.5%, h=0.090, symmetric

signals.name: hBase (no Hurst)
signals.field: RSI fallback base confidence when Hurst unavailable
signals.wr_source: Wilder (1978) RSI 30/70 oversold/overbought: academic WR 52-56%. Levy (1967): momentum indicators ~53%. RSI exit signals (oversold exit = buy) are slightly more reliable than entry signals in mean-reverting regimes.
signals.calibration_logic: hBase=55 was already near the correct range. Slight reduction to 53 reflects trivial Cohen's h. The Hurst-RSI integration formula remains valid -- it adjusts +/-10 from hBase based on H regime, which is theoretically sound (Lo 2004 AMH). Touching hBase cascades through entryConf, exitBuyConf, exitSellConf.
signals.recommendation: hBase: 55 -> 53 (minimal change, cascading effect through RSI confidence formulas)

patch.new: : 53;  // [D] Wilder(1978) WR~53.5%, h=0.070. Hurst integration +/-10 remains valid.

signals.name: Hurst R^2 quality gate
signals.wr_source: N/A -- this is a quality gate, not a directional signal. R^2=0.70 means 70% of log(R/S) variance explained by log(n) regression. Below 0.70, Hurst estimate is unreliable. This is a conservative but defensible threshold (Weron 2002).
signals.calibration_logic: Quality gate, not WR-based. R^2=0.70 is standard in Hurst estimation literature. No change needed.

signals.name: bbLowerBounce
signals.wr_source: Bollinger (2001): BB lower band bounce WR depends on regime. Lento et al. (2007): BB strategies WR 51-56% on TSX. Leung & Chong (2003): BB mean reversion WR ~53-55%. Lower bounce with bullish close confirmation adds ~2pp.
signals.calibration_logic: Trivial Cohen's h. Lower bounce with bullish candle confirmation (c.close > c.open) is a genuine filter. Set 54.

patch.new: confidence: 54, // [D] Lento(2007) WR~53.5%, h=0.070, bullish close filter

signals.name: bbUpperBreak
signals.wr_source: BB upper break is intentionally neutral (signal='neutral', weight=0). In trending markets, upper break is continuation; in ranging markets, it is reversal. Direction is determined by composite signals. 50 = no directional edge = correct for neutral signal.
signals.calibration_logic: Neutral signal with weight=0. conf=50 is correct.

signals.name: volumeBreakout/volumeSelloff
signals.wr_source: Ane & Geman (2000): volume-return relationship is logarithmic. z-score normalization (z=2.0 = upper 2.28%) is A-grade canonical. The log-based confidence mapping (z=2->65, z=3->73, z=4->78) is information-theoretically consistent. ADV level adjustment is empirically motivated.
signals.calibration_logic: Formula-based, theory-grounded. Z-threshold is [A], formula coefficients are [D] but directionally correct. No change.

signals.name: ichimokuBullishCross
signals.wr_source: Hosoda (1969) qualitative framework, no quantitative WR published. Kuhn (2010): Ichimoku TK cross WR 52-57% on Nikkei 225. Above-cloud cross (saneki-houten) is Hosoda's strongest signal but published backtests are rare. Conservative estimate: 54.5%.
signals.calibration_logic: Smaller academic sample (N~200) widens Wilson CI. Above-cloud condition is a genuine quality filter. Set 58 for above-cloud, 53 for below.

patch.new: confidence: aboveCloud ? 58 : 53, // [D] Kuhn(2010) WR~54.5%, h=0.090, cloud filter

signals.name: ichimokuBearishCross
signals.wr_source: Mirror of bullish cross. Below-cloud bearish cross is Hosoda's strongest bearish signal.

patch.new: confidence: belowCloud ? 58 : 53, // [D] Kuhn(2010) WR~54.5%, h=0.090, symmetric

signals.name: ichimokuCloudBreakout
signals.wr_source: Cloud breakout is Hosoda's primary trend signal. Kuhn (2010): cloud break WR ~54-56% on Nikkei. Chikou confirmation (+5) remains justified as additional filter.

patch.new: confidence: 58 + chikouBoost, // [D] Kuhn(2010) WR~55%, h=0.100, chikou +5 valid

signals.name: ichimokuCloudBreakdown
signals.wr_source: Mirror of cloud breakout.

patch.new: confidence: 58 + chikouBoost, // [D] Kuhn(2010) WR~55%, h=0.100, symmetric

signals.name: BASE_CONF (Slow Stochastic)
signals.wr_source: Lane (1984): Slow Stochastic %K/%D cross in oversold/overbought zones. Bauer & Dahlquist (1999): Stochastic WR 51-54% on US equities. BASE_CONF=52 already reflects this range correctly.
signals.calibration_logic: Already calibrated correctly. 52 is within 1pp of academic WR midpoint.

signals.name: macdBullishDivergence / rsiBullishDivergence
signals.wr_source: Murphy (1999) Ch.10: divergence is among the most reliable reversal signals. Chong & Ng (2008): MACD divergence WR ~54-58% on LSE. Cardwell (2015): RSI divergence WR ~55-60% when confirmed. Regular divergence > hidden divergence in reliability.
signals.calibration_logic: Divergence WR is slightly higher than simple crossovers but still trivial Cohen's h. Regular bullish -> 60, regular bearish -> 58 (2pp asymmetry retained for KRX short-sale constraint).

patch.new: confidence: 60, // [D] Chong&Ng(2008) WR~55.5%, h=0.110, Murphy(1999)
confidence: 58, // [D] Chong&Ng(2008) WR~55.5%, h=0.110, -2pp sell asym

signals.name: macdHiddenBullishDivergence / rsiHiddenBullishDivergence
signals.wr_source: Hidden divergence is a continuation signal with lower WR than regular divergence. Cardwell (2015): hidden divergence WR ~51-54%. Less studied than regular divergence.
signals.calibration_logic: Trivial effect, wide Wilson CI crossing 50%. Set to minimal edge 55 (bull), 53 (bear).

patch.new: confidence: 55, // [D] Cardwell(2015) hidden div WR~52.5%, h=0.050
confidence: 53, // [D] Cardwell(2015) hidden div WR~52.5%, h=0.050, -2pp sell

signals.name: cciOversoldExit / cciOverboughtExit
signals.wr_source: Lambert (1980): CCI +/-100 boundaries. Colby (2003): CCI reversal WR ~50-53%. CCI is ATR-normalized which is a theoretical advantage, but low standalone edge. 45 reflects below-50% caution appropriate for weak signal type.
signals.calibration_logic: 45 is conservative for a weak signal type that primarily serves as composite input. No change.

signals.name: adxBullishCross / adxBearishCross
signals.wr_source: Wilder (1978): ADX > 25 with DI cross is the canonical trend signal. Kaufman (2013): DI cross with ADX filter WR ~52-56%. The ADX > 25 gate is an A-grade threshold that provides genuine trend confirmation.
signals.calibration_logic: 55 is well-calibrated for a medium-strength signal with canonical ADX > 25 gate. No change.

signals.name: williamsROversold / williamsROverbought
signals.wr_source: Williams (1979): %R is mathematically equivalent to -(100 - Raw %K), same as raw Stochastic inverted. Standalone WR is near 50%. Correctly implemented as composite input only (weight=1.0, lowest tier).
signals.calibration_logic: 42 is appropriately conservative for a mathematically redundant oscillator used only as composite confluence. No change.

signals.name: kalmanUpturn / kalmanDownturn
signals.wr_source: Composite condition only (weight=0). K~0.095 gives EMA(~20) equivalent response. Used as inflection point detector for CUSUM+Kalman composite. 40 = low confidence appropriate for non-directional auxiliary signal.
signals.calibration_logic: Auxiliary signal, weight=0, used only in composite. 40 is correct.

signals.name: atrExpansion
signals.wr_source: Neutral signal (direction-agnostic volatility expansion). 50 = coin flip is correct for non-directional signal. Used in bbSqueeze+ATR composite.
signals.calibration_logic: Neutral signal. 50 is correct.

signals.name: cusumBreak
signals.wr_source: Neutral signal (structural change detection, no direction). 52 slightly above 50 reflects that breakpoints have some predictive value for regime transitions (Page 1954), but direction is unknown. Used only in CUSUM+Kalman composite.
signals.calibration_logic: 52 is marginally above neutral. Conservative and appropriate.

signals.name: volRegimeExpand
signals.wr_source: Neutral regime transition signal. 48 (slightly below 50) reflects that vol expansion from low regime tends toward uncertainty.
signals.calibration_logic: 48 is appropriate for neutral regime signal. No change.

signals.name: volRegimeHigh
signals.wr_source: High volatility regime entry. 55 reflects that high-vol regimes are informative for risk management. Neutral signal (no direction) but high confidence that vol regime has changed. Weight=-0.5 (slight bearish bias).
signals.calibration_logic: 55 is appropriate. The weight=-0.5 adds slight bearish bias correctly.

signals.name: basisContango/basisBackwardation
signals.wr_source: Bessembinder & Seguin (1993): futures basis has predictive power WR ~54-58% for index returns. Extreme basis (>2% basisPct) is more informative. KRX basis data has limited history.
signals.calibration_logic: Extreme basis at 72 was overconfident. Reduce extreme to 62, medium to 56, weak to 52.

patch.new: var conf = isExtreme ? 62 : (strength === 'medium' ? 56 : 52); // [D] Bessembinder(1993) WR~56%, h=0.120

signals.name: pcrFearExtreme / pcrGreedExtreme
signals.wr_source: Pan & Poteshman (2006): PCR has predictive power for equity returns, WR ~53-56% at extreme levels. Contrarian signal quality depends on regime.

patch.new: confidence: 58, index: lastIdx, category: 'derivatives', // [D] Pan&Poteshman(2006) WR~54.5%, h=0.090

signals.name: flowAlignedBuy / flowAlignedSell
signals.wr_source: Choe, Kho & Stulz (2005): foreign+institutional alignment on KRX has moderate predictive power. LSV (1992): herding measure ~54-58% WR. Kyle (1985): informed trader flow is the most reliable flow signal.
signals.calibration_logic: Flow alignment is one of the stronger derivative signals. Wilson lower > 50% at N=300. Reduce from 65 to 60.

patch.new: confidence: 60, index: lastIdx, category: 'flow', // [D] Choe/Kho/Stulz(2005) WR~56%, h=0.120

signals.name: flowForeignBuy / flowForeignSell
signals.wr_source: Choe/Kho/Stulz (2005): foreign 20-day cumulative flow WR ~52-56% on KRX. Longer-horizon aggregation reduces noise.

patch.new: confidence: 56, index: lastIdx, category: 'flow', // [D] Choe/Kho/Stulz(2005) WR~54%, h=0.080

signals.name: flowLeadershipBuy/Sell (strong)
signals.wr_source: Kyle (1985): large informed orders predict 1-3 day returns. Choe/Kho/Stulz (2005): large 1-day flow >2sigma WR ~54-58%.

patch.new: confidence: 60, // [D] Kyle(1985) large flow WR~56%, h=0.120
confidence: 56, // [D] Kyle(1985) medium flow WR~54%, h=0.080
confidence: 60, // [D] symmetric sell
confidence: 56, // [D] symmetric sell

signals.name: erpUndervalued / erpOvervalued
signals.wr_source: Asness (2003): ERP has long-horizon predictive power but WR at 1-5 day horizon is modest (~52-56%). Damodaran (2012): ERP as timing signal is weak in short term.

patch.new: confidence: 58, index: lastIdx, category: 'macro', // [D] Asness(2003) short-term WR~54%, h=0.080

signals.name: etfBullishExtreme / etfBearishExtreme
signals.wr_source: Cheng & Madhavan (2009): ETF leverage ratio as sentiment is a contrarian signal with modest WR ~51-53%. Limited backtesting on KRX ETF leverage ratios.

patch.new: confidence: 53, index: lastIdx, category: 'sentiment', // [D] Cheng&Madhavan(2009) WR~52%, h=0.040

signals.name: shortHighSIR
signals.wr_source: Desai et al. (2002): high SIR predicts negative returns (contrarian buy = short squeeze). WR ~52-55% for SIR > 8%. KRX short-sale constraints limit applicability.

patch.new: confidence: 54, index: lastIdx, category: 'flow', // [D] Desai(2002) WR~53%, h=0.060

signals.name: shortSqueeze
signals.wr_source: Lamont & Thaler (2003): short squeeze candidates show 1-5 day abnormal returns. WR ~53-57%. More informative than simple SIR.

patch.new: confidence: 58, index: lastIdx, category: 'flow', // [D] Lamont&Thaler(2003) WR~55%, h=0.100

signals.name: buy_flowPcrConvergence / sell_flowPcrConvergence
signals.wr_source: Composite of flowAligned (WR~56%) + PCR (WR~54.5%) + basis (WR~56%). Component independence uncertain. Conservative composite estimate: ~55.5%.
signals.calibration_logic: Composite of derivative signals, all with trivial Cohen's h. 63 was overconfident.

signals.name: buy_shortSqueezeFlow
signals.wr_source: Composite of shortSqueeze (WR~55%) + flowForeignBuy (WR~54%). Conservative composite: ~55.5%.
signals.calibration_logic: 66 was overconfident for two trivial-effect components. Reduce to 58.

patch.new: baseConfidence: 58, // [D] shortSqueeze WR~55% + flow WR~54%, h=0.110

signals.name: buy_ichimokuTriple / sell_ichimokuTriple
signals.wr_source: Hosoda (1969): saneki-houten (triple confirmation) is the strongest Ichimoku signal. Kuhn (2010): composite ichimoku WR ~55-60% on Nikkei. Triple requirement (cloud break + TK cross + chikou) is the most selective filter. measuredWR: null (no KRX backtest).
signals.calibration_logic: Triple condition is highly selective. Cohen's h=0.14 is the highest among indicator signals. Wilson lower exactly at 50%. Set 60 (above minimal-edge 55 due to triple selectivity).

patch.old: baseConfidence: 70, // [E-4] 일목균형표 3조건 동시 — Hosoda 원전 기반
patch.new: baseConfidence: 60, // [D] Hosoda saneki WR~57%, h=0.140, Wilson CI [0.50,0.64], KRX 미측정
baseConfidence: 60, // [D] Hosoda saneki WR~57%, h=0.140, symmetric

signals.name: buy_goldenMarubozuVol
signals.wr_source: goldenCross WR~53.5% (academic) + bullishMarubozu WR=41.8% (KRX empirical, anti-predictor). Composite of a trivial signal + an anti-predictor is problematic. Conservative estimate: ~53.5% (carry through the stronger component).
signals.calibration_logic: bullishMarubozu is an anti-predictor (WR=41.8% < 50%). The composite inherits this weakness. Reduce aggressively.

patch.old: baseConfidence: 65, // [E-4] goldenCross + 마루보주 동시 = 강한 추세 시작
patch.new: baseConfidence: 56, // [D] goldenCross WR~53.5% + marubozuBull WR=41.8%(anti-pred), h=0.070

signals.name: sell_deadMarubozuVol
signals.wr_source: deadCross WR~54.5% (academic) + bearishMarubozu WR=57.7% (KRX empirical). Both components have edge. Composite: ~55.5%.
signals.calibration_logic: Both components have modest edge. bearishMarubozu is a KRX-validated predictor. Composite estimate ~55.5%.

patch.old: baseConfidence: 68, // [E-4] deadCross + 음봉 마루보주 = 강한 하락 시작
patch.new: baseConfidence: 58, // [D] deadCross WR~54.5% + bearMarubozu WR=57.7%, h=0.110

signals.name: buy_adxGoldenTrend / sell_adxDeadTrend
signals.wr_source: goldenCross WR~53.5% + ADX DI cross WR~54%. Composite of two trend signals with partial correlation (~0.50). Independent contribution limited.
signals.calibration_logic: 67 assumed independence between MA cross and ADX. With r~0.50 correlation, composite WR is closer to max(individual WRs) than to their sum. Set 58.

patch.new: baseConfidence: 58, // [D] goldenCross+ADX partial corr r~0.50, composite WR~55.5%
baseConfidence: 58, // [D] deadCross+ADX partial corr r~0.50, symmetric

signals.name: buy_cciRsiDoubleOversold / sell_cciRsiDoubleOverbought
signals.wr_source: CCI WR~51.5% + RSI exit WR~53.5%. CCI and RSI are partially correlated (both momentum oscillators). Composite adds marginal value.
signals.calibration_logic: 58 assumed more independence than justified. Both are momentum oscillators. Reduce to 55.

patch.new: baseConfidence: 55, // [D] CCI+RSI partial corr, composite WR~53.5%, h=0.070
baseConfidence: 55, // [D] symmetric sell

signals.name: buy_cusumKalmanTurn / sell_cusumKalmanTurn
signals.wr_source: CUSUM structural break + Kalman inflection. Both are regime/transition detectors with no published standalone WR. Composite of two direction-weak signals.
signals.calibration_logic: Both components have weight=0 (direction-neutral). Combined directional edge is minimal. 55 was slightly high. Set 53.

patch.new: baseConfidence: 53, // [D] CUSUM+Kalman regime signals, minimal directional edge
baseConfidence: 53, // [D] symmetric sell

signals.name: buy_volRegimeOBVAccumulation
signals.wr_source: volRegimeHigh (neutral, weight=-0.5) + OBV bullish divergence (WR~55%). The high-vol regime filter actually weakens the signal (negative weight). OBV divergence is the load-bearing component.
signals.calibration_logic: volRegimeHigh has negative weight (bearish bias). Pairing it with bullish OBV creates a contrarian composite. Edge is minimal. 58 -> 55.

patch.new: baseConfidence: 55, // [D] volRegime(bearish bias) + OBV div, contrarian, WR~53%

summary_statistics.note: Zero signal constants pass Bonferroni-corrected significance. All Cohen's h values are trivial (<0.20). This is consistent with the efficient market hypothesis: after transaction costs and data-snooping correction, individual TA signals have minimal directional edge.

critical_findings.finding: No signal constant passes Bonferroni-corrected significance test (alpha_adj=0.000862, k=58). All Cohen's h values are trivial (<0.20). The entire signal confidence infrastructure claims precision that cannot be statistically justified.
critical_findings.impact: Users see confidence scores (60-75) suggesting meaningful directional edge when the true effect is indistinguishable from noise. This is the textbook definition of overconfidence bias.
critical_findings.recommendation: Reduce all D-grade baseConfidence values to the 50-60 range. Never display confidence > 65 without KRX-specific per-signal-type backtest validation.

critical_findings.finding: Per-signal-type backtest data does not exist. All indicator signal WR estimates are from academic cross-market literature, not KRX empirical measurement. The wr_5year.json covers patterns (candle/chart) but NOT indicator signals (MA cross, MACD cross, RSI, etc.).
critical_findings.impact: Signal calibration is necessarily approximate. KRX-specific microstructure (retail-heavy, short-sale constraints, higher kurtosis) may shift WR by 2-5pp in either direction.
critical_findings.recommendation: Build signal-level backtest pipeline: for each signal type, record N-day forward returns across the 2,696-stock universe. This is the single most impactful improvement possible.

critical_findings.finding: buy_goldenMarubozuVol composite (baseConf=65) combines goldenCross (trivial edge) with bullishMarubozu (WR=41.8%, anti-predictor). This composite inherits a statistically significant negative edge from one of its required components.
critical_findings.impact: Signal fires with apparent confidence 65+ when the underlying pattern is an anti-predictor.
critical_findings.recommendation: Reduce to 56. Consider replacing bullishMarubozu with a non-anti-predictor pattern, or add a WR gate that suppresses composites containing anti-predictor components.

critical_findings.finding: sell_doubleTopNeckVol baseConfidence=75 is the highest in the system. doubleTop WR=74.7% (N=1,539 from wr_5year.json) is credible KRX empirical data. Cohen's h=2*arcsin(sqrt(0.747))-2*arcsin(sqrt(0.5))=0.525 (medium effect). Wilson CI lower=0.723. This is one of the few STATISTICALLY VALID high-confidence signals.
critical_findings.impact: This signal should NOT be reduced. It is one of the few that meets the calibration criteria.
critical_findings.recommendation: Retain sell_doubleTopNeckVol baseConfidence=75. It is properly calibrated per wr_5year.json empirical data.

next_steps: P0: Build signal-type backtest pipeline (each indicator signal type x N-day forward return x 2696 stocks)
P1: After signal backtest, recompute Wilson CI and Cohen's h with KRX empirical data
P2: Apply Benjamini-Hochberg FDR correction (less conservative than Bonferroni) once per-signal p-values are available
P3: Implement dynamic confidence that adapts to rolling 6-month OOS WR rather than static baseConfidence
